batch_summary: In an apartment living room, Joey and Ross try to convince Frank to embrace the single life, but Frank insists that being with Alice, the person he truly cares about, is far better. Ross bitterly reflects on how he ruined his deep connection with Rachel by sleeping with someone else. Joey expresses a poignant longing for a meaningful relationship of his own, wondering why he cannot find it. Reassured by Frank's conviction, Joey encourages Frank to hold onto his love for Alice, ending the conversation with a supportive hug.
cumulative_summary: In an apartment living room, Chandler briefly enters to return Joey's keys before Joey and Ross engage in a candid conversation with Frank about relationships. Joey and Ross initially encourage Frank to enjoy his youth and sleep with multiple women. However, Frank defends monogamy, arguing that intimacy is far better with someone you truly care about, comparing his relationship with Alice to what Ross once had with Rachel. This prompts a somber admission from Ross, who confesses he ruined his relationship with Rachel by sleeping with someone else. Joey, touched by Frank's description of true love, expresses his own vulnerability and frustration at not being able to find a deep connection. Frank reassures Joey that such love exists, prompting Joey to warmly congratulate Frank and advise him to hold onto his relationship with Alice as the two share a supportive hug.

batch_summary: Joey and Ross share their wedding roles with Phoebe, with Joey as best man and Ross as ring bearer, while Chandler makes a bizarre, towel-clad appearance. Meanwhile, Phoebe lures Alice over under the pretense of a laundry emergency, only to bluntly ask her not to marry Frank. Elsewhere, Monica confronts Pete in his high-tech office over an outrageously large check he left her, accusing him of trying to buy her affection.

However, Phoebe remains deeply concerned about her brother's relationship. She lures Alice to her apartment under the pretense of a laundry emergency, only to drop the act and bluntly ask Alice not to marry Frank. Meanwhile, Monica confronts the wealthy Pete in his high-tech office. Armed with an outrageously large check he left her as a tip, Monica demands to know if he is trying to buy her affection or if this is simply how he gets women to go out with him.

batch_summary: In his office, Pete charms Monica by explaining the large check was a tactic to break their chef-customer dynamic. Despite her initial reluctance to encourage his behavior, Monica softens when Pete sincerely asks her out for just one meal. Later, Phoebe returns home to find a dejected Frank hiding under her laundry. Frank reveals he is having a terrible day, hinting at the fallout from Phoebe's confrontation with Alice.
cumulative_summary: Following a heartfelt conversation about monogamy, Joey and Ross share their wedding roles with Phoebe. However, deeply concerned about her brother's relationship, Phoebe lures Alice to her apartment and bluntly asks her not to marry Frank. Meanwhile, Monica confronts the wealthy Pete in his office over an outrageously large check he left as a tip. Pete disarms her by explaining the check was a playful tactic to break their chef-customer dynamic. He sincerely confesses his feelings and convinces a hesitant Monica to agree to a single date. Later, Phoebe returns to her dark apartment and is startled to find a dejected Frank hiding under her clean laundry. Frank sadly reveals he is having a terrible day, hinting at the immediate and painful fallout from Phoebe's interference in his relationship with Alice.

batch_summary: Frank reveals that Alice called off the wedding because he is "too young." Devastated, Frank explains how Alice was the only source of happiness in his troubled life. Overcome with guilt upon seeing his immense grief, Phoebe confesses that she interfered and convinced Alice to end the relationship. Frank is shocked and deeply hurt, pointing out that even his mother's opposition to their relationship never resulted in such painful sabotage.

batch_summary: Frank expresses his deep disappointment in Phoebe's betrayal before admitting he has nowhere else to go. Meanwhile, Monica prepares for her date with the wealthy Pete. Stunned but charmed, Monica playfully tries to contribute to the bill.

Alice gently explains to a heartbroken Frank that despite their deep love, their age difference means they will want different things in the future, making their relationship unsustainable.

batch_summary: Despite Alice's attempts to break things off with Frank due to their age difference, the couple's intense passion quickly overrides their logic. They share a passionate embrace and fall onto the couch, prompting a defeated Phoebe to leave them alone. Meanwhile, Chandler listens to the subliminal stop-smoking tape in bed. However, the tape has been hijacked by Joey, who recorded messages urging Chandler to make him cheese sandwiches and buy him expensive pants, leaving Chandler bewildered.
